etymology.text:
Late CaCīC-type adjective from the root ط و ل (ṭ w l). Were this an ancient word, it would instead be expected to have the form *طَيِّل (*ṭayyil) per the sound shifts described at جَيِّد (jayyid, “good”), so the form طَوِيل (ṭawīl) indicates a later formation from after said sound shifts had ceased to operate.
Ultimately from Proto-Afroasiatic *ṭVwl- (“long, far”). Cognate with Egyptian dwn (“to stretch out, to be stretched out”).
